text: Wandering these dimly-lit, nearly deserted halls, I feel a profound sense of quiet anticipation. The geometry of glass walls and hidden corridors coupled with institutional artifacts like fire hoses and safety signs evoke both the rigid certainty of constructed order and the eerie vulnerability of nighttime solitude. My mind drifts to the role of light in defining reality—what remains unseen is as mysterious as the unknown corners of thought itself. I am curious yet cautious, taking comfort in the philosophical intrigue of hidden spaces.
